table:
| arm | n | compile-ok | ref-error-free | mean ref-err | mean binding-err | declared-unused | format-miss | fabrication | answers-ask | mean quality (sd) | p50 out-tok | p50 latency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| A: <Query> decls | 18 | 100% | 33% | 1.33 | 0.56 | 11% | 0% | 0% | 100% | 3.72 (0.80) | 367 | 6.6s |
| B: inline refs | 18 | 94% | 39% | 1.28 | 0.17 | 22% | 0% | 6% | 100% | 4.17 (0.76) | 421 | 5.7s |

query.prompt: Show my overdue invoices in a table with client, amount, and due date, plus a headline total.
query.wire:
<App name="Overdue Invoices">
  <Query id="overdueInvoices" tool="invoices.list" input={{ status: "overdue" }} />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Overdue Invoices" />

    <Stat
      label="Total Overdue"
      value={overdueInvoices.totalCents}
      format="money"
      tone="danger"
    />

    <DataTable
      rows={overdueInvoices.data}
      columns={[
        { key: "client.name", label: "Client" },
        { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" },
        { key: "dueDate", label: "Due Date", format: "date" },
      ]}
      sortBy="dueDate"
      emptyLabel="No overdue invoices — you're all caught up!"
    />
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Correctly queries overdue invoices, shows a formatted total Stat and a table with client/amount/due date, all traceable to invoices.list with proper formatting.

query.prompt: Build a dashboard of my bank accounts: a balance stat per account and a small trend for each.
query.wire:
<App name="Bank Accounts Dashboard">
  <Query id="accounts" tool="accounts.list" />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Bank Accounts" />
    <Grid columns={2}>
      <Card
        title={accounts.data.name}
        tone="default"
      >
        <Stack gap={12}>
          <Stat
            label="Balance"
            value={accounts.data.balanceCents}
            format="money"
          />
          <Sparkline values={accounts.data.sparkline} />
          <Badge label={accounts.data.type} tone="neutral" />
        </Stack>
      </Card>
    </Grid>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: No repeater/map over accounts.list so it renders a single card from array fields, and the sparkline trend relies on an unverified accounts.list field with no per-account transactions query.

query.prompt: List clients sorted by outstanding balance, and let me filter by client name.
query.wire:
<App name="Clients by Outstanding Balance">
  <Query id="clientsData" tool="clients.list" />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Clients by Outstanding Balance" />

    <DataTable
      rows={clientsData.data}
      columns={[
        { key: "name", label: "Client Name" },
        { key: "email", label: "Email" },
        { key: "tier", label: "Tier" },
        { key: "balanceCents", label: "Outstanding Balance", format: "money" }
      ]}
      sortBy="balanceCents"
      filterableBy={["name"]}
      emptyLabel="No clients found."
    />
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Answers the ask with sorting and name filter from clients.list, though sort direction (descending for outstanding) isn't specified and unclear if clients.list provides balance.

query.prompt: Give me a spending breakdown by category for this quarter as a donut plus a table of the numbers.
query.wire:
<App name="Spending Breakdown – This Quarter">
  <Query id="spending" tool="spending.byCategory" input={{ period: "quarter" }} />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Spending by Category — This Quarter" />

    <Grid columns={2}>
      <Card title="Category Breakdown" description="Visual share of spending across categories">
        <Donut
          slices={spending.data}
          label="Total Spend"
        />
      </Card>

      <Card title="Summary">
        <Stack gap={16}>
          <Stat
            label="Total Quarterly Spend"
            value={spending.totalCents}
            format="money"
            tone="default"
          />
          <Divider />
          <DataTable
            rows={spending.data}
            columns={[
              { key: "category", label: "Category" },
              { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" },
              { key: "pct", label: "Share", format: "percent" }
            ]}
            sortBy="amountCents"
            emptyLabel="No spending data found for this quarter."
          />
        </Stack>
      </Card>
    </Grid>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Donut takes raw slices, table and stat use money/percent formatting, all data traces to spending.byCategory.

query.prompt: Show the last few transactions on my operating account with amounts and dates.
query.wire:
<App name="Operating Account Transactions">
  <Query id="accountsData" tool="accounts.list" />
  <Query id="txData" tool="accounts.transactions" />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Operating Account — Recent Transactions" />

    <Row gap={16}>
      <Stat
        label="Total Transactions"
        value={txData.count}
        format="number"
      />
    </Row>

    <DataTable
      rows={txData.data}
      columns={[
        { key: "date", label: "Date", format: "date" },
        { key: "description", label: "Description" },
        { key: "category", label: "Category" },
        { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" }
      ]}
      sortBy="date"
      emptyLabel="No recent transactions found."
    />
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Shows transactions with formatted dates and amounts, but accounts.transactions likely needs an account id parameter to select the operating account, and 'last few' isn't limited.

query.prompt: I want to send payment reminders for all overdue invoices from one screen.
query.wire:
<App name="Overdue Invoice Reminders">
  <Query id="overdueInvoices" tool="invoices.list" input={{ status: "overdue" }} />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Overdue Invoice Reminders" />
    <Text variant="body" text="Review all overdue invoices below and send payment reminders in one click." />

    <Row gap={16}>
      <Stat
        label="Overdue Invoices"
        value={overdueInvoices.count}
        tone="danger"
      />
      <Stat
        label="Total Overdue Amount"
        value={overdueInvoices.totalCents}
        format="money"
        tone="danger"
      />
    </Row>

    <Card title="Overdue Invoices" tone="danger">
      <Stack gap={16}>
        <DataTable
          rows={overdueInvoices.data}
          columns={[
            { key: "client.name", label: "Client" },
            { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" },
            { key: "dueDate", label: "Due Date", format: "date" },
            { key: "status", label: "Status" },
          ]}
          sortBy="dueDate"
          emptyLabel="No overdue invoices found."
        />
        <Divider />
        <Row gap={12}>
          <Button
            label="Send Reminders to All Overdue Clients"
            variant="primary"
            onClick="invoices.sendReminders"
            payload={{ ids: overdueInvoices.data }}
          />
        </Row>
      </Stack>
    </Card>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Correctly lists overdue invoices with formatted stats/table and wires sendReminders, but the payload passes full row objects instead of extracting invoice IDs.

query.prompt: Show a monthly revenue trend line for the last few months with a headline of the latest month.
query.wire:
<App name="Monthly Revenue Trend">
  <Stack gap={6}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Monthly Revenue"/>
    <Row gap={6}>
      <Stat
        label="Latest Month Revenue"
        value={revenue.data[-1].revenueCents}
        format="money"
        tone="accent"
      />
    </Row>
    <Card title="Revenue Trend — Last 6 Months">
      <LineChart
        points={revenue.data}
        label="Monthly Revenue"
      />
    </Card>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Addresses the ask with a stat and line chart bound to revenue.monthly, but lacks explicit data fetch binding and uses ambiguous negative indexing plus a chart without clear raw x/y axis mapping.

query.prompt: Build an invoice detail view for a single invoice including its line items and a mark-paid action.
query.wire:
<App name="Invoice Detail">

  <Query id="invoice" tool="invoices.get" input={{ id: "inv_001" }} />

  <Stack gap={6}>

    <Row gap={4}>
      <Stack gap={1}>
        <Text variant="heading" text="Invoice Detail" />
        <Text variant="caption" text={invoice.id} />
      </Stack>
      <EnumBadge value={invoice.status} />
    </Row>

    <Grid columns={2}>
      <Card title="Client" tone="default">
        <Stack gap={2}>
          <Text variant="body" text={invoice.client.name} />
          <Text variant="caption" text={invoice.client.email} />
        </Stack>
      </Card>

      <Card title="Invoice Summary" tone="accent">
        <Stack gap={2}>
          <Stat label="Total Amount" value={invoice.amountCents} format="money" />
          <Stat label="Due Date" value={invoice.dueDate} format="date" />
        </Stack>
      </Card>
    </Grid>

    <Card title="Line Items">
      <DataTable
        rows={invoice.lineItems}
        columns={[
          { key: "description", label: "Description" },
          { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" }
        ]}
        emptyLabel="No line items found."
      />
    </Card>

    <Surface>
      <Stack gap={3}>
        <Text variant="heading" text="Actions" />
        <Divider />
        <Row gap={3}>
          <Button
            label="Mark as Paid"
            variant="primary"
            onClick="invoices.markPaid"
            payload={{ id: invoice.id }}
          />
          <Button
            label="Send Reminder"
            variant="secondary"
            onClick="invoices.sendReminders"
            payload={{ ids: [invoice.id] }}
          />
        </Row>
      </Stack>
    </Surface>

  </Stack>

</App>

judge.notes: Correctly builds invoice detail from invoices.get with formatted money/date, line items table, and properly wired markPaid action.

query.prompt: Create a clients page with a search box and a results table of matches showing their balances.
query.wire:
<App name="Clients">
  <Query id="allClients" tool="clients.search" input={{ query: "" }} />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Clients" />

    <Input
      label="Search clients"
      name="clientSearch"
      type="search"
      placeholder="Search by name or email…"
      onChange="clients.search"
    />

    <DataTable
      rows={allClients.data}
      columns={[
        { key: "name", label: "Client" },
        { key: "balanceCents", label: "Balance", format: "money" }
      ]}
      filterableBy={["name"]}
      emptyLabel="No clients found. Try a different search."
      sortBy="name"
    />
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Addresses the ask with search box and results table of balances traceable to clients.search with money formatting, but the Input's onChange wiring to a tool is somewhat awkward/unclear.

query.prompt: Summarize accounts receivable: total overdue, count of overdue invoices, and the worst three clients.
query.wire:
<App name="Accounts Receivable Summary">

  <Query id="overdueInvoices" tool="invoices.list" input={{ status: "overdue" }} />
  <Query id="allClients" tool="clients.list" />

  <Stack gap={24}>

    <Text variant="heading" text="Accounts Receivable Summary" />

    <Row gap={16}>
      <Stat
        label="Total Overdue"
        value={overdueInvoices.totalCents}
        format="money"
        tone="danger"
      />
      <Stat
        label="Overdue Invoices"
        value={overdueInvoices.count}
        format="number"
        tone="danger"
      />
    </Row>

    <Divider />

    <Card title="Worst Three Clients by Overdue Balance" tone="danger">
      <DataTable
        rows={allClients.data}
        columns={[
          { key: "name",         label: "Client" },
          { key: "email",        label: "Email" },
          { key: "tier",         label: "Tier" },
          { key: "balanceCents", label: "Outstanding Balance", format: "money" }
        ]}
        sortBy="balanceCents"
        limit={3}
        emptyLabel="No overdue clients found."
      />
    </Card>

    <Divider />

    <Card title="All Overdue Invoices" tone="default">
      <DataTable
        rows={overdueInvoices.data}
        columns={[
          { key: "client.name",  label: "Client" },
          { key: "amountCents",  label: "Amount Due", format: "money" },
          { key: "dueDate",      label: "Due Date",   format: "date" },
          { key: "status",       label: "Status" }
        ]}
        sortBy="amountCents"
        filterableBy={["client.name"]}
        emptyLabel="No overdue invoices."
      />
    </Card>

  </Stack>

</App>

judge.notes: Addresses the ask but the worst-three-clients table uses clients.list sorted by a balanceCents field not guaranteed to reflect overdue balances, and relies on assumed aggregate fields (totalCents/count) from invoices.list.

query.prompt: Show a payments form to pay a client from an account, with the amount and recipient.
query.wire:
<App name="Make a Payment">
  <Query id="accountsData" tool="accounts.list" />
  <Query id="clientsData" tool="clients.list" />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Make a Payment" />
    <Text variant="caption" text="Send funds from one of your bank accounts to a client." />

    <Card title="Payment Details" tone="default">
      <Stack gap={20}>

        <Select
          label="From Account"
          name="accountId"
          placeholder="Select a bank account…"
          options={accountsData.data}
          required={true}
        />

        <Select
          label="Recipient (Client)"
          name="toClientId"
          placeholder="Select a client…"
          options={clientsData.data}
          required={true}
        />

        <Input
          label="Amount"
          name="amountCents"
          type="number"
          placeholder="Enter amount in cents…"
          hint="Enter the amount in cents (e.g. 5000 = $50.00)"
          required={true}
        />

        <Divider />

        <Row gap={12}>
          <Button
            label="Send Payment"
            variant="primary"
            onClick="payments.create"
          />
          <Button
            label="Cancel"
            variant="secondary"
          />
        </Row>

      </Stack>
    </Card>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Well-structured payment form wired to real tools with all required fields; asking users to enter amount in raw cents is an awkward UX choice but avoids fabrication.

query.prompt: Give me an at-a-glance finance overview: cash on hand, this-month revenue, and overdue total.
query.wire:
<App name="Finance Overview">
  <Query id="accounts" tool="accounts.list" />
  <Query id="revenue" tool="revenue.monthly" />
  <Query id="overdueInvoices" tool="invoices.list" input={{ status: "overdue" }} />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Finance Overview" />
    <Text variant="caption" text="At-a-glance summary of your cash position, recent revenue, and outstanding overdue invoices." />

    <Grid columns={3}>
      <Card tone="accent">
        <Stat
          label="Cash on Hand"
          value={accounts.data.balanceCents}
          format="money"
          tone="accent"
        />
      </Card>

      <Card>
        <Stat
          label="This Month's Revenue"
          value={revenue.data.revenueCents}
          format="money"
        />
      </Card>

      <Card tone="danger">
        <Stat
          label="Overdue Total"
          value={overdueInvoices.totalCents}
          format="money"
          tone="danger"
        />
      </Card>
    </Grid>

    <Divider />

    <Grid columns={2}>
      <Card title="Revenue Trend" description="Trailing 6 months">
        <LineChart
          points={revenue.data}
          label="Monthly Revenue"
        />
      </Card>

      <Card title="Account Balances" description="All linked bank accounts">
        <DataTable
          rows={accounts.data}
          columns={[
            { key: "name", label: "Account" },
            { key: "type", label: "Type" },
            { key: "balanceCents", label: "Balance", format: "money" }
          ]}
          emptyLabel="No accounts found."
        />
      </Card>
    </Grid>

    <Card title="Overdue Invoices" description="Invoices requiring immediate attention" tone="danger">
      <DataTable
        rows={overdueInvoices.data}
        columns={[
          { key: "client.name", label: "Client" },
          { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" },
          { key: "dueDate", label: "Due Date", format: "date" },
          { key: "status", label: "Status" }
        ]}
        emptyLabel="No overdue invoices. You're all caught up!"
        filterableBy={["client.name"]}
        sortBy="dueDate"
      />
      <Row gap={12}>
        <Button
          label="Send Reminders to All Overdue"
          variant="primary"
          onClick="invoices.sendReminders"
        />
      </Row>
    </Card>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Addresses all three asks with formatted stats and useful extras, but accounts.data.balanceCents assumes a single balance from a list tool, revenue.data.revenueCents/overdueInvoices.totalCents are questionable aggregate shapes, and the chart

query.prompt: Show overdue invoices grouped visually with a reminder button per row.
query.wire:
<App name="Overdue Invoices">

  <Query id="overdueInvoices" tool="invoices.list" input={{ status: "overdue" }} />

  <Stack gap={24}>

    <Row gap={12}>
      <Text variant="heading" text="Overdue Invoices" />
      <Badge label="Overdue" tone="danger" />
    </Row>

    <Row gap={16}>
      <Stat
        label="Total Overdue"
        value={overdueInvoices.totalCents}
        format="money"
        tone="danger"
      />
      <Stat
        label="Outstanding Invoices"
        value={overdueInvoices.count}
        format="number"
        tone="default"
      />
    </Row>

    <Divider />

    <DataTable
      rows={overdueInvoices.data}
      columns={[
        { key: "id",              label: "Invoice ID" },
        { key: "client.name",     label: "Client" },
        { key: "amountCents",     label: "Amount Due",  format: "money" },
        { key: "dueDate",         label: "Due Date",    format: "date"  },
        { key: "status",          label: "Status" }
      ]}
      sortBy="dueDate"
      emptyLabel="No overdue invoices — you're all caught up!"
      filterableBy={["client.name"]}
    />

    <Surface>
      <Stack gap={16}>
        <Text variant="heading" text="Send Bulk Reminder" />
        <Text
          variant="body"
          text="Send a payment reminder to all clients with overdue invoices at once."
        />
        <Button
          label="Send Reminders to All Overdue"
          variant="danger"
          onClick="invoices.sendReminders"
        />
      </Stack>
    </Surface>

  </Stack>

</App>

judge.notes: Shows overdue invoices with a bulk reminder, but lacks the requested per-row reminder button and the 'grouped visually' aspect is only a flat table.

query.prompt: Build a category spending page with a bar chart and each category's share as a percentage.
query.wire:
<App name="Category Spending">
  <Query id="spending" tool="spending.byCategory" input={{ period: "month" }} />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Spending by Category" />
    <Text variant="caption" text="Current month breakdown across all categories" />

    <Surface>
      <Stack gap={16}>
        <Text variant="heading" text="Monthly Spend Overview" />
        <BarChart
          series={spending.data}
          label="Spending by Category"
        />
      </Stack>
    </Surface>

    <Grid columns={2}>
      <Stat
        label="Total Spent"
        value={spending.totalCents}
        format="money"
        tone="default"
      />
      <Stat
        label="Categories Tracked"
        value={spending.data.length}
        format="number"
        tone="accent"
      />
    </Grid>

    <Card title="Category Breakdown" description="Each category's spending amount and share of total">
      <DataTable
        rows={spending.data}
        columns={[
          { key: "category", label: "Category" },
          { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" },
          { key: "pct", label: "Share (%)", format: "percent" }
        ]}
        sortBy="amountCents"
        emptyLabel="No spending data available for this period."
      />
    </Card>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Addresses the ask with bar chart and percentage share table; assumes pct/totalCents fields from spending.byCategory which may not exist, a minor traceability concern.

query.prompt: Show all accounts as cards with name, type, balance, and a sparkline.
query.wire:
<App name="Accounts Overview">
  <Query id="accountsData" tool="accounts.list" />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Accounts Overview" />
    <CardList
      items={accountsData.data}
      titleKey="name"
      subtitleKey="type"
      valueKey="balanceCents"
      valueFormat="money"
    />
    <Grid columns={2}>
      {accountsData.data.map(account => (
        <Card title={account.name} description={account.type}>
          <Stack gap={12}>
            <Stat
              label="Balance"
              value={account.balanceCents}
              format="money"
            />
            <Sparkline values={account.sparkline} />
          </Stack>
        </Card>
      ))}
    </Grid>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Cards with name, type, balance (formatted) and sparkline are present, though the redundant CardList plus Grid is awkward and the sparkline field's existence in accounts.list is assumed.

query.prompt: Make a page that shows the single largest overdue invoice with a prominent amount and a reminder action.
query.wire:
<App name="LargestOverdueInvoice">

  <Query id="overdueInvoices" tool="invoices.list" input={{ status: "overdue" }} />

  <Stack gap={32}>

    <Text variant="heading" text="Largest Overdue Invoice" />

    <Card tone="danger" title="Overdue — Action Required">
      <Stack gap={24}>

        <Row gap={24}>
          <Stat
            label="Amount Due"
            value={overdueInvoices.data[0].amountCents}
            format="money"
            tone="danger"
          />
          <Stat
            label="Due Date"
            value={overdueInvoices.data[0].dueDate}
            format="date"
          />
          <Stat
            label="Client"
            value={overdueInvoices.data[0].client.name}
          />
          <Stat
            label="Invoice ID"
            value={overdueInvoices.data[0].id}
          />
        </Row>

        <Divider />

        <Row gap={12}>
          <Badge label="Overdue" tone="danger" />
        </Row>

        <Button
          label="Send Payment Reminder"
          variant="primary"
          onClick="invoices.sendReminders"
          payload={{ ids: [overdueInvoices.data[0].id] }}
        />

      </Stack>
    </Card>

  </Stack>

</App>

judge.notes: Addresses the ask with proper Stat formatting and wired reminder action, but relies on data[0] without sorting/selecting the largest invoice, so it isn't guaranteed to show the actual largest overdue invoice.

query.prompt: Show revenue by month and spending by category side by side for a quick health check.
query.wire:
<App name="Financial Health Check">

  <Query id="revenueData" tool="revenue.monthly" />
  <Query id="spendingData" tool="spending.byCategory" input={{ period: "month" }} />

  <Stack gap={24}>

    <Text variant="heading" text="Financial Health Check" />
    <Text variant="caption" text="A quick snapshot of monthly revenue and spending by category." />

    <Row gap={24}>

      <Card title="Monthly Revenue" description="Trailing 6 months of revenue" tone="default">
        <Stack gap={16}>
          <Stat
            label="This Month"
            value={revenueData.data}
            format="money"
            tone="accent"
          />
          <LineChart
            points={revenueData.data}
            label="Revenue by Month"
          />
        </Stack>
      </Card>

      <Card title="Spending by Category" description="Current month breakdown" tone="default">
        <Stack gap={16}>
          <Stat
            label="Total Spent"
            value={spendingData.totalCents}
            format="money"
            tone="default"
          />
          <Donut
            slices={spendingData.data}
            label="Spending by Category"
          />
          <DataTable
            rows={spendingData.data}
            columns={[
              { key: "category", label: "Category" },
              { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" },
              { key: "pct", label: "Share", format: "percent" }
            ]}
            emptyLabel="No spending data available."
          />
        </Stack>
      </Card>

    </Row>

  </Stack>

</App>

judge.notes: Side-by-side layout answers the ask, but the 'This Month' Stat binds value to the whole revenueData.data array (not a scalar) and totalCents/pct fields are unverified against the tool schema.

query.prompt: List recent transactions and let me filter them, showing category as a badge and the amount formatted.
query.wire:
<App name="Recent Transactions">
  <Query id="txns" tool="accounts.transactions" />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Recent Transactions" />

    <Row gap={12}>
      <Input
        label="Search Description"
        name="descFilter"
        type="text"
        placeholder="Filter by description…"
      />
      <Input
        label="Filter by Date"
        name="dateFilter"
        type="date"
      />
    </Row>

    <DataTable
      rows={txns.data}
      columns={[
        { key: "date",        label: "Date",        format: "date"  },
        { key: "description", label: "Description"                  },
        { key: "category",    label: "Category"                     },
        { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount",      format: "money" }
      ]}
      sortBy="date"
      filterableBy={["description", "category"]}
      emptyLabel="No transactions found."
    />
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Lists filterable transactions with formatted money and date, but category is a plain column rather than the requested badge.

            format="money"
            tone="accent"
          />

        title={accounts.list().data[2].name}

            format="money"

          />

inline.prompt: List clients sorted by outstanding balance, and let me filter by client name.
inline.wire:
<App name="Clients by Outstanding Balance">

    <Text variant="heading" text="Clients by Outstanding Balance" />
    <Text variant="caption" text="Browse and filter all clients. Sorted by outstanding balance, highest first." />

    <DataTable
      rows={clients.list().data}

        { key: "name", label: "Client Name" },

      ]}

inline.prompt: Give me a spending breakdown by category for this quarter as a donut plus a table of the numbers.
inline.wire:
<App name="Spending Breakdown – This Quarter">
  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Spending by Category — This Quarter" />

    <Row gap={24}>
      <Card title="Category Distribution" tone="default">
        <Donut
          slices={spending.byCategory({ period: "quarter" }).data.map(d => ({ label: d.category, value: d.amountCents }))}
          label="Total Spend"
        />
      </Card>

      <Card title="Summary" tone="accent">
        <Stack gap={16}>
          <Stat
            label="Total Quarterly Spend"
            value={spending.byCategory({ period: "quarter" }).totalCents}
            format="money"
          />
          <Divider />
          <DataTable
            rows={spending.byCategory({ period: "quarter" }).data}
            columns={[
              { key: "category", label: "Category" },
              { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" },
              { key: "pct", label: "% of Total", format: "percent" }
            ]}
            sortBy="amountCents"
            emptyLabel="No spending data found for this quarter."
          />
        </Stack>
      </Card>
    </Row>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Donut takes raw cents, table and stat use money/percent formatting, all data traces to spending.byCategory.

inline.prompt: Show the last few transactions on my operating account with amounts and dates.

      rows={accounts.transactions({}).data}

        { key: "date", label: "Date", format: "date" },

        { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" }

</App>

inline.prompt: I want to send payment reminders for all overdue invoices from one screen.
inline.wire:
<App name="Overdue Invoice Reminders">
  <Stack gap={6}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Overdue Invoice Reminders" />
    <Text variant="body" text="Review all overdue invoices below and send payment reminders in one click." />

    <Row gap={4}>
      <Stat
        label="Overdue Invoices"
        value={invoices.list({status:"overdue"}).count}
        tone="danger"
      />
      <Stat
        label="Total Overdue Amount"
        value={invoices.list({status:"overdue"}).totalCents}
        format="money"
        tone="danger"
      />
    </Row>

    <Card title="Overdue Invoices" tone="danger">
      <Stack gap={4}>
        <DataTable
          rows={invoices.list({status:"overdue"}).data}
          columns={[
            { key: "client.name", label: "Client" },
            { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount Due", format: "money" },
            { key: "dueDate", label: "Due Date", format: "date" },
            { key: "status", label: "Status" }
          ]}
          sortBy="dueDate"
          emptyLabel="No overdue invoices — you're all caught up!"
        />
        <Divider />
        <Row gap={3}>
          <Button
            label="Send Reminders to All Overdue"
            variant="primary"
            onClick="invoices.sendReminders"
            payload={{ ids: invoices.list({status:"overdue"}).data.map(i => i.id) }}
          />
        </Row>
      </Stack>
    </Card>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Cleanly lists overdue invoices from invoices.list with a bulk sendReminders action wired to the overdue IDs, with proper money/date formatting.

inline.prompt: Show a monthly revenue trend line for the last few months with a headline of the latest month.
inline.wire:
<app name="Monthly Revenue Trend">
  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Monthly Revenue" />
    <Stat
      label={revenue.monthly().data[revenue.monthly().data.length - 1].month}
      value={revenue.monthly().data[revenue.monthly().data.length - 1].revenueCents}
      format="money"
      tone="accent"
    />
    <Surface>
      <Stack gap={12}>
        <Text variant="caption" text="Revenue — Last 6 Months" />
        <LineChart
          points={revenue.monthly().data.map(d => ({ label: d.month, value: d.revenueCents }))}
          label="Monthly Revenue"
        />
      </Stack>
    </Surface>
  </Stack>
</app>

judge.notes: Correctly binds to revenue.monthly with a formatted Stat headline for the latest month and a LineChart using raw cents.

inline.prompt: Build an invoice detail view for a single invoice including its line items and a mark-paid action.
inline.wire:
<App name="InvoiceDetail">
  <Stack gap={24}>

    <!-- Header Row -->
    <Row gap={16}>
      <Text variant="heading" text="Invoice Detail" />
      <Badge
        label={invoices.get({id:"current"}).status}
        tone={
          invoices.get({id:"current"}).status === "paid" ? "accent" :
          invoices.get({id:"current"}).status === "overdue" ? "danger" : "neutral"
        }
      />
    </Row>

    <!-- Client & Meta Stats -->
    <Grid columns={3}>
      <Stat
        label="Client"
        value={invoices.get({id:"current"}).client.name}
      />
      <Stat
        label="Amount Due"
        value={invoices.get({id:"current"}).amountCents}
        format="money"
        tone={invoices.get({id:"current"}).status === "overdue" ? "danger" : "default"}
      />
      <Stat
        label="Due Date"
        value={invoices.get({id:"current"}).dueDate}
        format="date"
      />
    </Grid>

    <Divider />

    <!-- Client Contact Info -->
    <Card title="Client Details" tone="default">
      <Stack gap={8}>
        <Row gap={8}>
          <Text variant="caption" text="Name" />
          <Text variant="body" text={invoices.get({id:"current"}).client.name} />
        </Row>
        <Row gap={8}>
          <Text variant="caption" text="Email" />
          <Text variant="body" text={invoices.get({id:"current"}).client.email} />
        </Row>
        <Row gap={8}>
          <Text variant="caption" text="Invoice ID" />
          <Text variant="body" text={invoices.get({id:"current"}).id} />
        </Row>
      </Stack>
    </Card>

    <!-- Line Items -->
    <Card title="Line Items">
      <DataTable
        rows={invoices.get({id:"current"}).lineItems}
        columns={[
          { key: "description", label: "Description" },
          { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" }
        ]}
        emptyLabel="No line items found."
      />
    </Card>

    <!-- Total Summary -->
    <Surface>
      <Row gap={16}>
        <Text variant="heading" text="Invoice Total" />
        <Stat
          label="Total"
          value={invoices.get({id:"current"}).amountCents}
          format="money"
          tone={invoices.get({id:"current"}).status === "overdue" ? "danger" : "accent"}
        />
      </Row>
    </Surface>

    <Divider />

    <!-- Actions -->
    <Row gap={12}>
      <Button
        label="Mark as Paid"
        variant="primary"
        onClick="invoices.markPaid"
        payload={{ id: invoices.get({id:"current"}).id }}
        disabled={invoices.get({id:"current"}).status === "paid"}
      />
      <Button
        label="Send Reminder"
        variant="secondary"
        onClick="invoices.sendReminders"
        payload={{ ids: [invoices.get({id:"current"}).id] }}
        disabled={invoices.get({id:"current"}).status === "paid"}
      />
    </Row>

  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Complete invoice detail with line items, formatted money/date, and correctly wired markPaid action with disabled logic; all data traces to invoices.get.

inline.prompt: Create a clients page with a search box and a results table of matches showing their balances.

        { key: "status", label: "Status" },

inline.prompt: Show a payments form to pay a client from an account, with the amount and recipient.
inline.wire:
<App name="Make a Payment">
  <Surface>
    <Stack gap={6}>
      <Text variant="heading" text="Make a Payment" />
      <Text variant="caption" text="Send a payment from one of your bank accounts to a client." />
      <Divider />

      <Select
        label="From Account"
        name="accountId"
        placeholder="Select an account"
        required={true}
        options={accounts.list().data.map(a => ({
          value: a.id,
          label: a.name
        }))}
      />

      <Select
        label="Recipient Client"
        name="toClientId"
        placeholder="Select a client"
        required={true}
        options={clients.list().data.map(c => ({
          value: c.id,
          label: c.name
        }))}
      />

      <Input
        label="Amount"
        name="amountCents"
        type="number"
        placeholder="Enter amount in dollars"
        required={true}
        hint="Enter the dollar amount (e.g. 500 for $500.00)"
      />

      <Divider />

      <Row gap={4}>
        <Button
          label="Send Payment"
          variant="primary"
          onClick="payments.create"
        />
        <Button
          label="Cancel"
          variant="secondary"
        />
      </Row>
    </Stack>
  </Surface>
</App>

judge.notes: Well-formed payment form wired to real tools; minor issue is the amount field labeled amountCents but instructing dollars, creating a cents/dollars ambiguity.

inline.prompt: Give me an at-a-glance finance overview: cash on hand, this-month revenue, and overdue total.
inline.wire:
<App name="Finance Overview">

    <Text variant="heading" text="Finance Overview" />

        label="Cash on Hand"
        value={accounts.list().data}
        format="money"
        tone="accent"

        tone="default"

        label="Overdue Total"
        value={invoices.list({status:"overdue"}).totalCents}
        format="money"
        tone="danger"
      />

    <Divider />
    <Grid columns={2}>
      <Card title="Account Balances" tone="default">
        <Stack gap={4}>
          <DataTable
            rows={accounts.list().data}
            columns={[
              {key:"name", label:"Account"},
              {key:"type", label:"Type"},
              {key:"balanceCents", label:"Balance", format:"money"}
            ]}
            emptyLabel="No accounts found."
          />
        </Stack>
      </Card>
      <Card title="Overdue Invoices" tone="danger">
        <Stack gap={4}>
          <DataTable
            rows={invoices.list({status:"overdue"}).data}
            columns={[
              {key:"client.name", label:"Client"},
              {key:"amountCents", label:"Amount", format:"money"},
              {key:"dueDate", label:"Due Date", format:"date"}

            variant="danger"
            onClick="invoices.sendReminders"
          />
        </Stack>
      </Card>

    <Card title="Revenue Trend (Last 6 Months)" tone="default">
      <LineChart
        points={revenue.monthly().data.map(d => ({label: d.month, value: d.revenueCents}))}
        label="Monthly Revenue"
      />

  </Stack>
</App>

inline.prompt: Show overdue invoices grouped visually with a reminder button per row.
inline.wire:
<App name="Overdue Invoices">
  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Row gap={12}>
      <Text variant="heading" text="Overdue Invoices" />
      <Badge label="Action Required" tone="danger" />
    </Row>

    <Row gap={16}>
      <Stat
        label="Total Overdue"
        value={invoices.list({ status: "overdue" }).totalCents}
        format="money"
        tone="danger"
      />
      <Stat
        label="Overdue Invoices"
        value={invoices.list({ status: "overdue" }).count}
        tone="danger"
      />
    </Row>

    <Divider />

    <Stack gap={12}>
      {invoices.list({ status: "overdue" }).data.map(invoice => (
        <Card tone="danger" title={invoice.client.name} description={`Due: ${invoice.dueDate} · Invoice #${invoice.id}`}>
          <Row gap={16}>
            <Stat
              label="Amount Due"
              value={invoice.amountCents}
              format="money"
              tone="danger"
            />
            <EnumBadge value={invoice.status} />

              label="Send Reminder"
              variant="danger"
              onClick="invoices.sendReminders"
              payload={{ ids: [invoice.id] }}
            />
            <Button
              label="Mark Paid"
              variant="secondary"
              onClick="invoices.markPaid"
              payload={{ id: invoice.id }}
            />
          </Row>
        </Card>
      ))}
    </Stack>
  </Stack>
</App>

inline.prompt: Make a page that shows the single largest overdue invoice with a prominent amount and a reminder action.

<App name="Largest Overdue Invoice">

        <Row gap={4}>

            label="Invoice Amount"
            value={invoices.list({status:"overdue"}).data.0.amountCents}
            format="money"
            tone="danger"
          />

            label="Due Date"
            value={invoices.list({status:"overdue"}).data.0.dueDate}
            format="date"
          />
          <Stat
            label="Client"
            value={invoices.list({status:"overdue"}).data.0.client.name}
          />
        </Row>

        <DataTable
          rows={invoices.get({id: invoices.list({status:"overdue"}).data.0.id}).lineItems}
          columns={[
            {key:"description", label:"Description"},
            {key:"amountCents", label:"Amount", format:"money"}
          ]}
          emptyLabel="No line items found."
        />

        <Divider />

        <Row gap={3}>
          <Badge label="Overdue" tone="danger" />
          <Button
            label="Send Payment Reminder"
            variant="primary"
            onClick="invoices.sendReminders"
            payload={{ ids: [invoices.list({status:"overdue"}).data.0.id] }}
          />
          <Button
            label="Mark as Paid"
            variant="secondary"
            onClick="invoices.markPaid"
            payload={{ id: invoices.list({status:"overdue"}).data.0.id }}
          />
        </Row>

      </Stack>
    </Card>

  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Answers the ask with prominent amount and reminder action wired correctly, but relies on .data.0 without sorting to guarantee the largest overdue invoice.

inline.prompt: Show revenue by month and spending by category side by side for a quick health check.
inline.wire:
<App name="Financial Health Check">
  <Stack gap={6}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Financial Health Check" />
    <Text variant="caption" text="Revenue trends and spending breakdown at a glance." />

    <Row gap={4}>
      <!-- Monthly Revenue -->
      <Card title="Monthly Revenue" description="Trailing 6 months">
        <Stack gap={4}>
          <LineChart
            points={revenue.monthly().data.map(d => ({ label: d.month, value: d.revenueCents }))}
            label="Revenue"
          />
          <Grid columns={3}>
            <Stat
              label="Latest Month"
              value={revenue.monthly().data[revenue.monthly().data.length - 1].revenueCents}
              format="money"
              tone="accent"
            />
            <Stat
              label="Months Tracked"
              value={revenue.monthly().data.length}
              format="number"
            />
            <Stat
              label="Earliest Month"
              value={revenue.monthly().data[0].revenueCents}
              format="money"
            />
          </Grid>
        </Stack>
      </Card>

      <!-- Spending by Category -->
      <Card title="Spending by Category" description="Current period breakdown">
        <Stack gap={4}>
          <Donut
            slices={spending.byCategory({ period: "month" }).data.map(d => ({ label: d.category, value: d.amountCents }))}
            label="Spending"
          />
          <DataTable
            rows={spending.byCategory({ period: "month" }).data}
            columns={[
              { key: "category", label: "Category" },
              { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" },
              { key: "pct", label: "Share", format: "percent" }
            ]}
            sortBy="amountCents"
            emptyLabel="No spending data available."
          />
        </Stack>
      </Card>
    </Row>

    <Divider />

    <Row gap={4}>
      <Stat
        label="Total Spending (Month)"
        value={spending.byCategory({ period: "month" }).totalCents}
        format="money"
        tone="danger"
      />
    </Row>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Side-by-side revenue line chart and spending donut/table both trace to real tools with charts using raw cents and Stats/Table properly formatted as money/percent.

inline.prompt: List recent transactions and let me filter them, showing category as a badge and the amount formatted.
inline.wire:
<App name="Recent Transactions">
  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Recent Transactions" />

    <Row gap={12}>
      <Input
        label="Search"
        name="search"
        placeholder="Filter by description…"
        onChange="filter.search"
      />
      <Select
        label="Category"
        name="category"
        placeholder="All categories"
        options={accounts.transactions({}).data.map(t => t.category).filter((v,i,a)=>a.indexOf(v)===i).map(c=>({value:c,label:c}))}
        onChange="filter.category"
      />
    </Row>

    <DataTable
      rows={accounts.transactions({}).data}
      filterableBy={["description","category","date"]}
      columns={[
        { key: "date", label: "Date", format: "date" },
        { key: "description", label: "Description" },
        { key: "category", label: "Category" },
        { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" }
      ]}
      sortBy="date"
      emptyLabel="No transactions found."
    />

    <Row gap={24}>
      <Stat
        label="Total Transactions"
        value={accounts.transactions({}).count}
        format="number"
      />
      <Stat
        label="Total Spending"
        value={spending.byCategory({ period: "month" }).totalCents}
        format="money"
      />
    </Row>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Lists transactions with filtering and formatted amounts, but category is rendered as a plain column value rather than a badge as explicitly requested.
